w20: Accelerator-optimized instance featuring dual NVIDIA A100 GPUs and substantial VRAM, designed for demanding AI/ML workloads requiring high parallel processing capabilities.
c150: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-2g: Accelerator-optimized instance with 2 NVIDIA A100 GPUs and 170GB RAM, ideal for demanding AI/ML and HPC tasks.
c200: Google Cloud Platform's a2-highgpu-2g offers robust acceleration with dual NVIDIA A100 GPUs, 80GB total VRAM, and 24 x86_64 vCPUs. Optimized for intensive AI/ML model training and high-throughput inference workloads.
w150: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-2g is an accelerator-optimized server featuring an x86_64 architecture powered by 24 Intel Xeon vCPUs across 12 physical cores operating at 2.2 GHz. The CPU includes L1d cache of 384 KB, L2 cache of 12 MB, and L3 cache of 38 MB. It is paired with 170 GB of memory. This instance bundles two NVIDIA A100 GPUs, providing a total of 80 GB of VRAM, with no local storage. While general CPU benchmarks, such as single-core stress-ng and gzip compression, are in the bottom 25%, and multi-core CPU performance is categorized as average, the server demonstrates top-tier performance for LLM inference. Specifically, it achieves 6477.28 tokens/sec for 135M model prompt processing and 22.39 tokens/sec for 70B model text generation. Memory bandwidth is reported as average. This configuration is primarily suited for demanding AI/ML model training, high-throughput LLM inference, and data science jobs that leverage significant GPU acceleration, rather than CPU-bound general computing tasks.
